Fred Thomas and John II. Guilty,
who told Judge NAM that VIII
liam McManus, the night clerk of
the Workingmen' Home. had
beaten them over the head with
stick of stove wood, were fined SO
each in police court yesterday aft
ernoon for disorderly conduct. Nc
Manus said that the men had been
very noisy in their preparations for
bed, which lasted nearly an hour,


Ernest Freebere and Thomas
whitley, recently implicated in the
. J. w. w. free speech fight, for
I. which they served Jail sentences,
pleaded guilty to the charge of
burglary before Judge Mann in the
police court yesterday afternoon.
The young men were warned by
the court that they We're putting
themselves in danger of the peni-
Tertiary, but they persisted in their
plea.


Charged with dsorderly conduct,
Ben VAIL lineman, and w. j.
Mckay, matchmaker, each "a
years of age, were arrested by
Offices Bradley and Goodwin yes
terday afternoon. Mckay gave
bail, but Vail was locked up. The
officers said the two men had been
fighting. Vail looked like he had
been through a sausage grinder.


Fifty new fire alarm boxes are
being advertised for by the city.
This means the entire Naange of
the switchboard arrangements at
the central fire station.


Louis r. Hachez, Albert E. Liv-
ingston and Charles e. Ammans
have incorporated the Pacific Real
ty & Loan company and the Mill
oki Investment company, each with
capitalization of $4,000.


E. r. Conan of the Exchange
bank has been elected to the pres
idency of the Washington Fire In
surance company of Seattle.


3. r. Lewis says that the B. r.
Lewis Lumber company will not
open a plant at Post Falls, as re-
ported.


Captain Archie McNab and Mrs.
Mary Mclaughlin, an aged couple,
who eloped from the Spokane cOun-
ty poor farm, were married at
Cranbrook, B. c., and will make
their home there. This move will
save Spokane county a dollar a
day.


w. r. Vickery of Kansas City
has succeeded G. D. Linn as chief
of the postal inspectors for Wash
ington, Idaho, Oregon and Alaska.
with headquarters at Spokane. &.
